# 05. Amazon fake review brokers

> **Use boundary:** This file is written for research, ethics, detection, and policy analysis. It describes mechanisms at a conceptual level so they can be studied, criticised, or detected. It is not an execution guide for manipulating platforms, audiences, investors, advertisers, or creators.


## Domain

Marketplace reviews

## Evidence type

Company enforcement / litigation reports

## What the case demonstrates

Fake review brokers recruit reviewers in exchange for money/free products, distorting ratings and product ranking.

## What is known

Amazon reported legal actions against brokers and social-media groups, including more than 10,000 Facebook groups and nearly 350,000 participants no longer incentivized through certain broker sites.

## What is not known / caution

Do not overstate beyond the source record. Distinguish documented enforcement, platform policy, reputable reporting, and unresolved allegations.

## Signal layers involved

- Visibility: whether the object appears widely seen or discussed.
- Credibility: whether observers infer independent validation.
- Conversion: whether apparent interest turns into sales, streams, installs, or signups.
- Attribution: whether the correct actor gets credit.
- Legitimacy: whether the signal becomes status or institutional trust.

## Ethical problem

The ethical issue is not simply that marketing exists. It is that the audience, platform, or market may infer genuine demand from signals whose origin, independence, or quality has been distorted.

## Evidence strength

Strong for existence of enforcement and market; source is Amazon, so pair with FTC/fake-review sources.

## Slide use

Use this case to show a concrete version of the thesis: a proxy signal was used as evidence, but the path by which that signal was produced complicates or corrupts the inference.

## Source notes

- **Amazon continues legal actions to protect customers from fake reviews** (Company enforcement / primary). Amazon reported legal actions against fake review brokers and said nearly 350,000 people using broker websites were no longer incentivized to write fake/misleading Amazon reviews. URL: https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/policy-news-views/amazon-continues-legal-actions-to-protect-customers-from-fake-reviews
- **Amazon targets fake review fraudsters on social media** (Company enforcement / primary). Amazon filed legal action against administrators of more than 10,000 Facebook groups allegedly orchestrating fake reviews in exchange for money/free products. URL: https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/policy-news-views/amazon-targets-fake-review-fraudsters-on-social-media
- **FTC final rule banning fake reviews and testimonials (2024)** (Regulator / primary). Prohibits fake/false reviews and testimonials, buying reviews conditioned on sentiment, review suppression, insider reviews without disclosure, and misuse of fake social-media indicators. URL: https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2024/08/federal-trade-commission-announces-final-rule-banning-fake-reviews-testimonials
